title: Amylase activity
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"599is","text":"This protocol is a scaled-down, modified version of the Enzymatic Assay of α-Amylase (EC 3.2.1.1) found here.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":104,"length":4}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"97CE4B00F8F411EBA3A807C483AFB954","url":"https://www.sigmaaldrich.com/NL/en/technical-documents/protocol/protein-biology/enzyme-activity-assays/enzymatic-assay-of-a-amylase"}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"eavm9","text":"All instances of dH2O can be replaced with ultrapure water.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":19,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"5c8j0","text":"Chemicals","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bikcd","text":"Potato starch","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"rnar","text":"D-(+)-maltose monohydrate","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"21dec","text":"Sodium phosphate monobasic","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"25tbk","text":"Sodium chloride","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ft13e","text":"potassium sodium tartrate, tetrahydrate","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2790m","text":"Sodium Hydroxide","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9gfos","text":"3,5 Dinitrosalicylic acid","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4dgj6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1bgac","text":"Equipment","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2jos4","text":"Pipettes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aj6sk","text":"2 -- 20 μL","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"gd53","text":"20 -- 200 μL","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cgjvu","text":"100 -- 1000 μL","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"26i4v","text":"8-channel pipette (ca. 20 - 300 μL) [OPTIONAL, but recommended]","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"14ch1","text":"96 well microplate","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cg27g","text":"Spectrophotometer (with 540 nm filter)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: 0.5% Potato starch solution add a small amount of dH 2 O to a beaker add 0.5 Mass Percent 0.5% (w/v) potato starch Boil for 00:15:00 while stirring Take off heat and leave at room temperature Add dH 2 O to final volume Continuous stirring throughout the assay
0.2% Maltose 1. add 100 mg maltose to 50 mL dH 2 O
Buffer add 250 mL dH 2 O to a beaker add 600 mg of sodium phosphate, monobasic add 97.5 mg of sodium chloride adjust to pH 6.9 with 1 M NaOH Contributed by users and 1 M HCl Contributed by users
2 M NaOH add 0.8 g NaOH Contributed by users to 10 mL dH 2 O

5.3 M potassium sodium tartrate, tetrahydrate solution add 14.96 mg Potassium sodium tartrate tetrahydrate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #S2377 to 10 mL of the 2 M NaOH solution dissolve solids with heat and stirring DO NOT heat to a boil
96 mM 3,5-Dinitrosalicylic acid solution add 0.438 g 3,5-dinitrosalicylic acid to 20 mL dH 2 O dissolve solids with heat and stirring DO NOT heat to a boil

Colour reagent add 12 mL of 50-70 °C dH 2 O to an appropriate size amber bottle (or something that can protect the solution from light). add (slowly and with mixing) 8 mL of warm 5.3 Molarity (M) Potassium sodium tartrate tetrahydrate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #S2377 add 20 mL of warm 96 mM 3,5-Dinitrosalicylic acid solution The colour reagent is stable for 6 months in a dark place at ambient temperature
Samples are prepared in a 96 deepwell plate with approx. 1 mL volume per well. Samples should be analysed in duplicates or triplicates.
Saliva samples are included as a positive control for amylase activity. Saliva donors rinse their mouth with water for 00:00:30

Multiskan FC (Microplate Photometer, Thermo Scientific, 51119000) Photometer settings: 540 nm filter Photometer reading 1 Pause Shake Pause Photometer reading 2 Make sure to include a sample blank (with dH 2 O for saliva positive controls, and stock artificial saliva for samples)
Prepare the standard curve by regressing (OLS) the ΔA 540 of each Standard on mg Maltose ( go to step #12 )
Calculate ΔA 540 of each Standard by subtracting the Standard Blank. \Delta A_{540 \space Standard} = \Delta A_{540 \space Standard} - \Delta A_{540 \space Standard Blank}
To each well, add 75 µL of sample (or saliva) It is best to make duplicates or triplicates of the samples

Add the following reagents to the wells of a deep-well microplate so each well contains a total volume of 225 μL. Each column in the table represents a single well. Add the Maltose to each well first, then dH 2 O, then colour reagent. Reagent STD1 STD2 STD3 STD4 STD5 STD6 STD7 STD Blank 1 0.2% Maltose 3.75 15 30 45 60 75 150 0 2 dH2O 146.25 135 120 105 90 75 0 150 3 Colour reagent 75 75 75 75 75 75 75 75 All quantities are in μL
Collect the saliva by spitting into 50 ml plastic centrifuge tubes.
Centrifuge the saliva at 1000 x g, °C, 10 minutes and sample from the supernatant.
Boil the deep-well microplate(s) for 00:03:00 , then cool on ice to room temperature.
Add 675 µL of dH 2 O to each well for a final volume of 900 µL .
go to step #12 and repeat the process, but adding artificial saliva instead of dH 2 O.

Then add 75 µL of the 0.5% potato starch solution to each of the wells with samples (and saliva) Once the starch has been added to the sample, the reaction will begin. The starch should be added quickly; ideally with a multichannel pipette .
Incubate the plate at 36 °C for 00:06:00

Remove the plate from the incubator and add 75 µL of colour reagent to the sample wells, then boil for 00:03:00 . The colour reagent will stop the reaction, so should be added with a multichannel pipette.
Cool the plate on ice to Room temperature then add 675 µL dH 2 O to each well for a final volume of 900 µL (homogenise the solution with the pipette). Use a new pipette tip for each sample.
then transfer 200 µL to a 96 well microplate suitable for the photometer.
Two standard curves are prepared: one containing dH 2 O, and one containing artificial saliva. Artificial saliva by Bjørn Peare Bartholdy
Calculate ΔA 540 of each Sample by subtracting the Sample Blank. \Delta A_{540 \space Sample} = \Delta A_{540 \space Sample} - \Delta A_{540 \space Sample Blank}
Then calculate the mg of Maltose released (x) using the regression coefficients: \frac{y - b}{a} = x Where y is the \Delta A_{540 \space Sample} , b is the intercept, and a is the slope.

Units per mL enzyme can be calculated as: U/mL \space enzyme = \frac{mg \space Maltose \space released \times dilution \space factor}{mL \space enzyme} where dilution factor is how much the sample was diluted (if at all), and mL enzyme is how much of the sample was added in step 17. go to step #17 A Unit is defined as mg of maltose released from starch in 6 minutes.
creator: Bjørn Peare Bartholdy
